title: Elderly person collapsed — possible stroke, caregiver calling
sentences: I need an ambulance right away, my patient has collapsed.
I'm his home caregiver, we're at 1203 Linden Avenue, it's a single-story house.
He's 78 years old and he just collapsed in the kitchen while we were talking.
He's breathing but barely responding, his face looks droopy on the left side.
I think he might be having a stroke, his speech was slurred before he went down.
He has a history of high blood pressure and atrial fibrillation.
I put him in the recovery position and I'm staying with him now, please hurry.
